title: Drovian
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg|canon=Slombo's species}}

|length=

{{Quote|They're killers you hear? Murderers. Take it from me, Wormhead. You're an outsider. Keep your nose in your own business.|Ism Oolos records an encounter with a Drovian in his journal|<ref name="egas" />}}
The '''Drovians''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that was bipedal in form, with corpulent bodies several times heavier than that of [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. Their four [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] were extremely strong tentacles, which terminated in three pincers. Their bodies were [[Color/Legends|grey]] or orange, with black or brown hair. Most Drovians developed an addiction to [[zwil]], originally an offworld cake flavoring agent.

The Drovians were native to [[Nim Drovis]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Meridian sector/Legends|Meridian sector]]. The species was split into two tribes, the [[Drovian (tribe)|Drovians]]—from whom the species name was derived—and the [[Gopso'o]]. Having developed into a network of pastoral communities, these two tribes were engaged in a vicious [[Nim Drovis Civil War|civil war]] that persisted for centuries, its original cause lost to [[Time/Legends|time]]. The first to make contact with the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], the Drovian tribe became ascendant, with higher education, income and technology levels. Several Drovians spread out into the galaxy, eventually appearing on worlds such as [[Jebble]], [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia]], involved in criminal endeavors, such as [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] gangs, [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] contests and working for underworld [[Crime lord/Legends|kingpin]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]. While a member of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], on several occasions, the Drovians found their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] located within [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] territory, such as during the [[Jedi Civil War]] and the [[New Sith Wars]].

Nim Drovis was part of the Imperial splinter faction known as the [[Greater Maldrood]], before joining the New Republic. The civil war came to a head in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]] during a [[Death Seed]] pandemic, when the [[Gopso'o]] tribe almost gained control of the planet while financially aided by the [[Loronar Corporation/Legends|Loronar Corporation]]. Eventually, emissaries from the [[New Jedi Order]] managed to peacefully resolve the conflict.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Not a particle of difference between them, you understand, except that they have been at blood feud for, literally, centuries.|Ism Oolos|<ref name="pot" />}}
[[File:Gopsoo-TEGtPaM.jpg|thumb|left|upright|A primitive Gopso'o tribesman, with braided hair]]
The [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] Drovian [[Species/Legends|species]] was bipedal in form, with corpulent bottom-heavy physiques.<ref name="egas" /> They averaged twice the weight of a standard [[Human/Legends|Human]] and were comparatively tall, averaging around two [[Meter/Legends|meters]]. Their stocky bodies were supported by two thick, trunk-like [[Limb/Legends|legs]]; a form of extremely strong tentacle. Each limb ended in three, sharp, articulated pincers.<ref name="pot" /> These could be either the same [[Color/Legends|color]] as the limbs themselves,<ref name="kotor" /> or a different shade.<ref name="Marked" />

Drovians breathed through membrane-lined breathing tubes on their necks, often covered in mucus. Through these, most Drovians would suck fist-sized plugs of raw [[zwil]]. Zwil was a yellow, cake flavoring agent from [[Algarian|Algarine cuisine]], smelling of cinnamon and [[Vanilla/Legends|vanilla]], that had a mild [[Spice/Legends|narcotic]] effect on Drovians.<ref name="egas" /> Zwil was introduced by offworld [[Merchant/Legends|traders]] and became so prevalent that virtually all Drovians were addicted to the substance.<ref name="pot" /> This led them to develop a more potent variation known as [[Drovian zwil]], which had a narcotic effect on other species too, such as the [[Shistavanen/Legends|Shistavanens]], and which soon became available on major [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] such as [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="ccw">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref>

Drovians had numerous sensory devices in their bodies, giving them exceptional aim and depth perception.<ref name="pot" /> Drovian skin pigmentation included [[gray]]<ref name="Marked" /> and [[orange]].<ref name="kotor" /> Tufts of dark hair grew from their heads, which was worn according to cultural affiliation.<ref name="egas" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|I made the mistake of asking a Drovian the reason for their animosity against the Gopso'o. The Drovian, whose temperament I'd usually describe as "agreeable," thrust me against the wall, pressing his sharp pincers hard into my chest.|Ism Oolos records an encounter with a Drovian in his journal|<ref name="egas" />}}
[[File:Equatorial_swamp.jpg|thumb|right|The equatorial swamps of Nim Drovis]]
Prior to contact with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the species had developed into a pastoral network of tribes. The two major tribes were the [[Drovian (tribe)|Drovians]]—from whom the species name was derived—and the [[Gopso'o]]. The tribes were separated by cultural differences, such as hair styles. The Drovian tribe cropped their hair short,<ref name="egas" /> while the Gopso'o tribe grew their hair in a long topknot. They braided into this topknot random shapes of colored [[Plastic/Legends|plastic]] and [[Rubber/Legends|rubber]] used as animal totems. Both tribes spoke [[Drovian (language)|Drovian]], though they had slightly different meanings for certain words. For instance, the word for truth was singular for one tribe and plural for the other.<ref name="pot" />

This difference in meaning was theorized as a possible origin for a [[Nim Drovis Civil War|civil war]] that broke out between the two tribes; however, the exact origins were lost to time as the conflict raged unresolved for [[Standard year/Legends|centuries]], with atrocities building up on both sides. The Drovian tribe gained a significant advantage over the Gopso'o by making first contact with the wider galaxy. Ultimately, they became the dominant tribe, with higher-level technology, and greater access to quality education and higher paid employment. The Drovians also adopted [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] as their lingua franca, leaving the more primitive Gopso'o to speak Drovian. When speaking either language, Drovian voices were nasal, and, coming as they did from esophaguses stuffed with zwil plugs, sounded bubbly.<ref name="pot" />

The civil war occupied a central and sensitive place in Drovian psychology, with every inhabitant of Nim Drovis, circa 14 ABY, knowing casualties of the war.<ref name="egpm">''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]''</ref> When questioned as to the origins of the conflict, otherwise agreeable Drovians were known to react violently, reciting litanies of grievances attributed to the other side. Alleged atrocities committed by the Gopso'o tribe included using molds to [[Torture/Legends|torture]] and kill captives. This burnt the bodies so badly that the corpses were unrecognizable.<ref name="egas" /> They were even believed by some Drovians to take pleasure in killing innocent family members of their enemies. During skirmishes, Gopso'o warriors often invoked the names of their murdered ancestors, shouted some of the [[Twenty-Five Personifications of Virtue]], such as Truth and Beauty, or yelled some other choice oath such as "''[[Drovian (language)|chunder]]''."<ref name="pot" />

Those members of the species not directly involved in the civil war occupied a range of positions. Gopso'o tribespeople worked on [[Slug (creature)/Legends|slugranches]]. These ranches produced bulk protein, which was purchased by interplanetary corporations. Some Drovians worked as professional mold-and-fungus removers, while others worked as small-time providers of goods and services. Members of the Drovian military were given three-[[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] rest breaks. These were often spent alongside assorted other Drovians consuming [[Alcohol/Legends|alcohol]], [[Spice/Legends|spice]], and other chemicals in local [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] such as the [[Wookiee's Codpiece]].<ref name="pot" />

By the time of the New Republic, the architectural style common on Nim Drovis was represented by heavy stone walls, supported by thick, wooden supports. Buildings also sported numerous balconies,<ref name="swe">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> plank gardens, and [[Bird/Legends|bird]] traps.<ref name="pot" /> The city of Bagsho, divided into a new and old town, contained numerous canals, spanned by wooden bridges. It included a slum known as the [[Thousand Stinking Ditches]], which was primarily occupied by Gopso'o. Their lower levels of education and lower paid work combined to confine them to such dilapidated areas.<ref name="egpm" />

==History==
===Galactic Republic===
[[File:Drovian_OR.jpg|thumb|left|Wargo, a Drovian living offworld in 4,000 BBY.]]
The Drovian species evolved on Nim Drovis,<ref name="egas" /> a planet located in the [[Meridian sector/Legends|Meridian sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], a [[Sector/Legends|sector]] located on the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] and one sector rimward of the border with the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="Outer Rim Extra">{{SWArchive|url=vault/books/atlas/news20090910/index.html|text=''Essential Atlas'' Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> The climate of Nim Drovis was hot and humid, with the planet prone to monsoons and flooding.<ref name="egas" /> Other species to evolve on the planet included [[Slug (creature)/Legends|slugs]], [[Marsh-gunny|marsh-gunnies]], [[gulper]]s, green-eyed [[wadie-platt]]s, reeds, [[Fungus/Legends|fungi]] and many forms of [[carnivorous mold]].<ref name="swe" /> The Drovians developed into an agrarian society, farming the slugs in ranches. As for the mobile carnivorous mold, many Drovians worked with acid and [[Flamethrower/Legends|flame-throwers]] to exterminate the [[Fungus/Legends|fungi]].<ref name="egas" />

Eventually, the Drovian species divided into two tribes, the eponymous Drovians and the Gopso'o. Civil war was sparked between the tribes, although the exact cause was soon lost to memory. The Drovian tribe was the first to make contact with the wider galaxy,<ref name="egas" /> sometime prior to [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]], by which stage Nim Drovis was considered part of the the "civilized" galaxy.<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref><ref name="Exploration">According to the "Galactic Explorations" map in ''The Essential Atlas'', exploration of the Meridian sector occurred in two distinct periods: The section nearest to the Perlemian Trade Route was explored between [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]] and [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]], while the remainder of the sector was not explored until between [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] and [[3000 BBY]]. It has not been revealed in which of these parts of the Meridian sector Nim Drovis is located.</ref> This provided the Drovian tribe with access to technology far in advance of their domestic capacity, which they soon translated into dominance over the Gopso'o.<ref name="egas" /> Contact with the galaxy had other effects: It produced immigration to Nim Drovis, with colonists from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]' planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] establishing a freeport on the world.<ref name="swe" /> It also saw the introduction of the narcotic zwil from Algar, to which eventually nearly all Drovians became addicted.<ref name="egas" />

Between [[3959 BBY]] and [[3956 BBY]], the Drovian homeworld was part of a large swath of the northeastern Outer Rim Territories that was absorbed into the growing territory of [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]], who invaded the Galactic Republic with a massive fleet constructed from an ancient, hidden factory known as the [[Star Forge]]. The Republic defeated the Sith Empire in 3956 BBY, and five years later scattered the [[Sith Triumvirate]] that had arisen to replace it. Following these victories, the Republic slowly began reintegrating its lost territory. Some [[1000 BBY/Legends|three-thousand]] years later, Nim Drovis again fell within [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] territory, this time during the conflict known as the [[New Sith Wars]]. This conflict ended in a climactic [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|battle]] on [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], with the Republic emerging victorious and the organized Sith hegemony collapsing.<ref name="Atlas" />

Under the Republic, a number of Drovians could be found offworld, such as the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Drovian, [[Silus (Drovian)|Silus]], who competed as a [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]] in the [[Unidentified Outer Rim planet (Silus)|Outer Rim]] in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]],<ref name="Marked" /> and several Drovians in the employ of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]].<ref name="bf4">''[[Boba Fett: Hunted]]''</ref> The next major galactic conflict facing the Republic, the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], also involved the Drovians' homeworld, which fell within one portion of the territory of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. The Confederacy sought to break away from what it perceived to be a corrupt Republic. The conflict lasted for three years, from [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] until [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], before the Confederacy was defeated and a new form of galactic government established: The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Atlas" />

===Galactic Empire===
The Empire was a centralized, pro-[[Human/Legends|Human]] administration. Its policies attracted opposition in the form of an [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which succeeded in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] in killing [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], and proclaimed itself the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. This defeat swiftly led to the once unitary Empire splitting into several feuding fragments. From 4 ABY until [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Nim Drovis was claimed as part of the [[Greater Maldrood]], under the control of Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Treuten Teradoc]]. Teradoc was convincingly defeated by Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] at both [[Battle of Maridun (8 ABY)|Maridun]] and [[Battle of Thanos|Thanos]], following which he abandoned his holdings and fled to the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. Imperial control of Nim Drovis and its surrounds was soon asserted by a new Imperial commander, [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]], who almost brought the New Republic to its knees only to be [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]] by his own [[Rukh/Legends|bodyguard]]. Nim Drovis then joined the New Republic.<ref name="Atlas" />

===New Republic===
{{Quote|I'm killin' those moldspawns because they killed my family, see? Because they disemboweled Garnu Hral Eschen, because they tore the flesh off the bones of the children of Ethras, because they…|Sergeant Knezex Hral Piksoar|<ref name="pot" />}}
[[File:Drovian_military_officer.jpg|thumb|right|upright|A Drovian military officer]]
Under the New Republic, Nim Drovis' largest freeport, Bagsho, became a link with the neutral planets of the Meridian sector.<ref name="swe" /> However, continuing unabated throughout these various galactic conflicts was the Drovian civil war. It came to a head in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]], when the [[Loronar Corporation/Legends|Loronar Corporation]] organized planetary rebellions on a number of planets, including Nim Drovis. Loronar had aligned with [[Seti Ashgad/Legends|Seti Ashgad]], a former-Imperial [[Senator/Legends|senator]] who had been made a political prisoner, who sought power in the Meridian sector. Loronar sided with the Gopso'o tribe, equipping them with [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] Loronar [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]]. After months of unrest, the Gopso'o launched a coordinated assault against the dominant Drovians, bombing public buildings and rumored to use [[Bilal gas|bilal]] and [[rush gas]] on civilian neighborhoods. The Gopso'o had numerous successes, including capturing a minor [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. Pinned down by the outbreak of the Death Seed plague and other conflicts in the Meridian sector, promised New Republic troops were unable to assist the beleaguered Drovians in Bagsho. Eventually, however, the Drovian government managed to drive back and subdue the attackers without Republic support.<ref name="pot" />

Afterwards, in amends for the lack of aid and the damage caused by the Death Seed plague, the New Republic took a greater interest in Nim Drovis. In addition to financing a weather station to control the incessant teeming rain—enabling the prolific molds to be controlled. This change allowed for slug ranching to became big business on Nim Drovis. [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] from the revitalized [[New Jedi Order]] also visited Nim Drovis during this period, and succeeded in brokering a historic peace accord between the Gopso'o and Drovian tribes. Nim Drovis also continued to host the [[Sector Medical Facility]]. In [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] species [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invaded]] the galaxy. As part of a convoluted plot to destroy the Jedi Order, the Yuuzhan Vong [[Elan]] and her familiar, [[Vergere]], defected to the Republic. They were initially transferred to the Nim Drovis Sector Medical Facility, where Republic [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Showolter]] took custody of the pair, before transferring them offworld. The pair's plot was foiled,<ref name="aoc">''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]''</ref> although Vergere later played a key role in the Yuuzhan Vong War.<ref name="T">''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]''</ref> By [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], virtually all the territory in the vacinity of Nim Drovis was in Yuuzhan Vong control. By [[137 ABY]], Nim Drovis was in territory controlled by [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Sith Empire]].<ref name="Atlas" /> At some point during this period, the [[Shi'ido]] [[Sentientologist|anthropologist]] [[Mammon Hoole]] included an entry on the Drovians in his publication ''[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''.<ref name="egas" />

==Drovians in the galaxy==
{{Quote|It seems that you're standing in the way of my destiny.|Silus threatens Darth Maul|<ref name="Marked" />}}
Since contact with the Galactic Republic, Drovians spread out into the galaxy, able to be found on a number of Outer Rim planets such as [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], and [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. The Drovians on Taris were part of the [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]], a notorious [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] in the [[Lower City]]. In [[3964 BBY]], they managed to apprehend the rogue [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]] and his [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] partner, [[Marn Hierogryph]], both marked with large [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] for the [[Padawan Massacre|murder]] of a number of Jedi Padawans. However, the pair successfully escaped from the gang members, tricking them into believing a stash of [[Spice/Legends|spice]] was nearby.<ref name="kotor" /> Later, [[Wargo]], one of those Drovian gang members, was conscripted into the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] forces on [[Jebble]].<ref name="kotor26">[[Knights of the Old Republic 26|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 26]]</ref>

[[File:Silus1.jpg|thumb|left|Silus, a Force-sensitive gladiator]]
Through their long war against the Gopso'o, many Drovians developed into tough fighters. A fine example of this was the Force-sensitive, pit-fighter Silus. Fighting in a gladiatorial competition, he combined his natural fighting abilities with [[The Force/Legends|Force]] tricks, killing numerous competitors such as the [[Aleena/Legends|Aleena]] [[Tabor (Aleena)|Tabor]] and [[Tabor's brother|his brother]]. When his [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark Force]] abilities threatened to alert the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], the Sith Lord Darth Sidious dispatched his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] to deal with him. In order to test Maul, the Drovian was offered the opportunity to displace Darth Maul if he could kill him. However, he proved to be no match for the Sith's abilities.<ref name="Marked" />

Another Drovian of this time was [[Kurjj]], who worked as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] for Jabba the Hutt. It was during this period that [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] first offered his services to the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]]. When Fett stood his ground against Jabba, Kurjj tried to kill Fett using a [[litch-knife]]. Warned by Kurjj's hot breath on his neck, the young [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] disarmed him, dropping him to the ground. After his defeat, Jabba condemned Kurjj to death by his pit beasts.<ref name="bf4" /> Jabba was known to employ a number of other Drovians as guards and servants, although none dared challenge Fett after Kurjj's death.<ref name="bf5">''[[Boba Fett: A New Threat]]''</ref> Drovians also operated on [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia]] as bounty hunters.<ref name="Dark Times: Blue Harvest, Part 1">[[Dark Times: Blue Harvest, Part 1|''Dark Times: Blue Harvest'', Part 1]]</ref>

Drovian soldiers included [[Ethras]], whose children had their flesh torn off by Gopso'o, and [[Garnu Hral Eschen]], who was disemboweled during the wars with the Gopso'o. Their descendant, [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Knezex Hral Piksoar]], worked for the New Republic on Nim Drovis. Piksoar and his squad greeted [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] when he landed in Bagsho with wounded from the New Republic [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] ''[[Corbantis]]''. Piksoar continued his family's fight against the Gopso'o, resisting their uprising in 13 ABY. When Solo ventured into the city to discover what has happening across the sector, Piksoar and his squad, together with [[Ism Oolos]], accompanied him. Encountering Gopso'o, and roving molds, they successfully escaped through the Thousand Stinking Ditches.<ref name="pot" />

During the Yuuzhan Vong War, many Drovians were employed at the Sector Medical Facility based in Nim Drovis' capital city of Bagsho, both as security guards and as secretaries. There, they aided Major Showolter and [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Saychel]] in interrogating Elan.<ref name="aoc" /> Later, in [[138 ABY]], a Drovian was present on [[The Wheel/Legends|The Wheel]], a Mid Rim [[Space station/Legends|space station]].<ref name="War">[[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Drovian species was created by [[Barbara Hambly]] for her novel ''[[Planet of Twilight]]'', published by [[Bantam Spectra]] in [[1997]]. Following this, the species received mentions in ''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'' and the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', both published in [[1998]], before Nim Drovis was again visited in [[2000]] in [[James Luceno]]'s ''[[The New Jedi Order: Agents of Chaos I: Hero's Trial]]''. While the former [[Star Wars: Essential Guides|''Essential Guide'']] had listed both Drovians and Gopso'o under Nim Drovis' species, the latter [[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']], published in [[2001]], clarified that the two were separate tribes of the same species.

Drovians made appearances in [[Elizabeth Hand]]'s ''[[Boba Fett: Hunted]]'' ([[2003]]) and ''[[Boba Fett: A New Threat]]'' ([[2004]]); the latter mistakenly spelled Drovian as "Drovion." A Drovian also featured in "[[Marked]]," a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[Rob Williams]] and pencilled by [[Cully Hamner]]. "Marked" was published in [[Star Wars Tales 24|''Star Wars Tales'' 24]] in [[2005]], and was later referenced in [[Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection 21|''Star Wars: The Official Starships & Vehicles Collection'' 21]]. Drovians then appeared in several arcs of [[John Jackson Miller]]'s [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] comic series. These were [[Knights of the Old Republic 3|''Commencement'']] ([[2006]]), [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Knights of Suffering|''Knights of Suffering'']] ([[2007]]) and [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Vector|''Vector'']] ([[2008]]).

In [[2009]], [[Randy Stradley]], under the pseudonym Mick Harrison, included a Drovian in ''[[Dark Times: Blue Harvest, Part 1]]'', while their homeworld was included in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''. This provided information on Nim Drovis' affiliation throughout a number of galactic conflicts.
